TESTIMONIALS.TITLE: What our clients say
TESTIMONIALS.SUBTITLE: Farms and cooperatives already growing smarter with AgroSync.

T1.QUOTE: We cut our water bill by nearly a third in the first season. The alerts alone paid for the system — we caught a broken valve before it wasted a full day of irrigation.
T1.NAME: Youssef Amrani

T2.QUOTE: As a cooperative managing dozens of hectares, visibility was our biggest problem. Now every plot reports in real time and our teams know exactly where to focus.
T2.NAME: Sophie Lemoine

T3.QUOTE: The automatic irrigation combined with weather forecasts means we stopped watering right before rainstorms. Simple change, real savings.
T3.NAME: Karim Bouzid

Q1.Q: Do I need technical skills to use AgroSync?
Q1.A: No. Installation and sensor setup are handled during deployment. Day-to-day use is a simple dashboard: view readings, control the pump, and check alerts.

Q2.Q: What hardware does the platform work with?
Q2.A: AgroSync is built around ESP32-based sensor nodes for soil moisture, temperature, and humidity, connected to standard irrigation pumps and valves.

Q3.Q: Does it work without a strong internet connection?
Q3.A: Sensor nodes tolerate intermittent connectivity and sync readings once back online. The dashboard always shows the last known state clearly labeled with its timestamp.

Q4.Q: Can I control irrigation manually as well as automatically?
Q4.A: Yes. You can switch any zone between manual control and automatic mode at any time, and adjust moisture thresholds and watering duration per zone.

Q5.Q: Who owns the collected field data?
Q5.A: You do. All sensor history, reports, and analytics belong to your farm and can be exported at any time.

Q6.A: Every plan includes onboarding support and continued assistance to keep your zones running smoothly.

ABOUT.TITLE: A connected platform for smarter agriculture
ABOUT.DESCRIPTION: AgroSync is an IoT platform that connects field sensors, weather data, and pump control into a single system. It helps farmers save water, protect crop health, and make informed decisions using real-time data.

TOOLTIPS.FARM_SUMMARY: A quick overview of your farm's current condition: crop, growth stage, irrigation mode and what to do next, all in one place.

TOOLTIPS.SOIL_MOISTURE: Shows the amount of available water in the soil. Low values may require irrigation, while optimal values indicate sufficient moisture.
TOOLTIPS.PUMP_STATUS: Indicates the current irrigation system state. Running means water is currently being supplied.
TOOLTIPS.WEATHER: Local weather conditions for your farm, used to help decide whether irrigation is needed today.
TOOLTIPS.AI_RECOMMENDATION: Provides intelligent irrigation advice based on current farm conditions.

ALERTS.LOW_SOIL_HUMIDITY_DESC: Soil moisture has dropped below the critical threshold in this zone.
ALERTS.LOW_SOIL_HUMIDITY_REC: Activate the pump manually or switch to automatic mode to start irrigation.
ALERTS.PUMP_ACTIVATED_DESC: The irrigation pump has been activated automatically based on soil conditions.
ALERTS.PUMP_ACTIVATED_REC: Monitor water consumption in the History page to track usage.
ALERTS.HIGH_TEMPERATURE_DESC: Air temperature has exceeded the recommended threshold. Crops may be under heat stress.
ALERTS.HIGH_TEMPERATURE_REC: Consider increasing irrigation frequency or scheduling watering during cooler hours.
ALERTS.SYSTEM_ERROR_DESC: A system error was detected. The device may not be operating correctly.
ALERTS.SYSTEM_ERROR_REC: Restart the ESP32 device and verify the network connection.
ALERTS.HEAVY_RAIN_DESC: Significant rainfall is forecast for this zone in the next few hours.
ALERTS.HEAVY_RAIN_REC: Consider postponing scheduled irrigation to conserve water and avoid over-saturation.
ALERTS.AUTO_IRRIGATION_STARTED_DESC: The system has automatically started irrigation based on low soil moisture readings.
ALERTS.AUTO_IRRIGATION_STARTED_REC: No action required. The system will stop automatically based on your duration settings.
ALERTS.AUTO_IRRIGATION_POSTPONED_DESC: Scheduled automatic irrigation was postponed due to rain forecast data.
ALERTS.AUTO_IRRIGATION_POSTPONED_REC: Monitor the weather forecast and resume irrigation manually if needed.
ALERTS.LOW_TANK_DESC: The water tank level has dropped to a critically low level and may run dry soon.
ALERTS.LOW_TANK_REC: Refill the water tank as soon as possible to ensure uninterrupted irrigation.
ALERTS.SENSOR_OFFLINE_DESC: The soil sensor is not transmitting data. Moisture readings are unavailable for this zone.
ALERTS.SENSOR_OFFLINE_REC: Check the sensor wiring and power supply, then restart the ESP32 device.
ALERTS.ESP32_DISCONNECTED_DESC: The ESP32 controller has lost its connection to the server. Remote control is unavailable.
ALERTS.ESP32_DISCONNECTED_REC: Verify the Wi-Fi network and power supply, then restart the ESP32 device.
ALERTS.IRRIGATION_START_DESC: An irrigation session has begun for this zone.
ALERTS.IRRIGATION_START_REC: No action required. The system will stop automatically based on your duration settings.
ALERTS.IRRIGATION_COMPLETE_DESC: The irrigation session for this zone has finished successfully.
ALERTS.IRRIGATION_COMPLETE_REC: Check the History page to review session details and water usage.
ALERTS.RAIN_SKIP_DESC: Scheduled irrigation was skipped because rainfall was detected or forecast for this zone.
ALERTS.RAIN_SKIP_REC: No action required. Irrigation will resume automatically when conditions are dry.
ALERTS.SAFETY_STOP_DESC: The irrigation pump was stopped automatically due to an abnormal sensor reading or safety condition.
ALERTS.SAFETY_STOP_REC: Inspect the sensor readings and pump status on the Pump Control page, then restart irrigation manually if it is safe to do so.

ABOUT.TITLE: About Smart Irrigation
ABOUT.SUBTITLE: What the platform does, how it works, and how to get the most out of it.

HERO.TITLE: Smart Irrigation
HERO.SUBTITLE: Smart Irrigation combines IoT soil sensors, live weather data, and rule-based recommendations to help you water only when and where it's needed — saving water while keeping crops healthy.

WORKFLOW.TITLE: How Smart Irrigation works
WORKFLOW.SUBTITLE: From the sensor in the soil to the pump in the field, every irrigation decision follows the same four-step path.
WORKFLOW.STEP1_DESC: An ESP32 device in the field measures soil moisture, temperature and air humidity and reports readings to the platform.
WORKFLOW.STEP2_DESC: The backend combines the latest sensor readings, local weather data, and recent irrigation history for the zone.
WORKFLOW.STEP3_DESC: In Automatic Mode, the system compares soil moisture to your configured threshold to decide whether to irrigate. In Manual Mode, you stay in full control instead.
WORKFLOW.STEP4_DESC: The pump relay is activated, the irrigation session is logged, and alerts are raised if something needs your attention.

FEATURES.TITLE: Everything you need to manage irrigation
FEATURES.SUBTITLE: Every card below links to a real, working page in the app.
FEATURES.DASHBOARD_DESC: A single-glance farm summary: live readings, crop health, weather and recent trends.
FEATURES.MONITORING_DESC: Real-time soil moisture, temperature and air humidity readings from your ESP32 device.
FEATURES.PUMP_DESC: Start or stop the pump manually, or switch to automatic threshold-based irrigation.
FEATURES.WEATHER_DESC: Local weather conditions on your Dashboard, used to inform irrigation decisions.
FEATURES.ALERTS_DESC: Notifications for low moisture, irrigation events, and safety stops.
FEATURES.HISTORY_DESC: A searchable log of sensor readings and pump activity over time.
FEATURES.ANALYTICS_DESC: Trends and water-usage statistics for your irrigation zone.
FEATURES.SETTINGS_DESC: Manage your account profile, password and language preferences.
FEATURES.AI_DESC: A rule-based tip on your Dashboard, informed by current soil, weather and alert data.

MODES.TITLE: Automatic or manual — you choose
MODES.SUBTITLE: Every zone can be switched between the two modes at any time from Pump Control.
MODES.AUTO_POINT1: You configure the soil moisture threshold and irrigation duration.
MODES.AUTO_POINT2: The system continuously monitors incoming sensor readings.
MODES.AUTO_POINT3: The pump starts automatically only when soil moisture drops below your threshold.
MODES.AUTO_POINT4: The pump stops once the configured duration is reached (with a safety check every few minutes as a backup).
MODES.AUTO_POINT5: You can disable Automatic Mode at any time and take back manual control.
MODES.MANUAL_POINT1: You start the pump yourself, whenever you decide irrigation is needed.
MODES.MANUAL_POINT2: You stop the pump yourself, whenever you decide it has run long enough.
MODES.MANUAL_POINT3: Automatic threshold-based decisions are ignored while Manual Mode is active.

GUIDE.TITLE: Using Smart Irrigation, step by step
GUIDE.SUBTITLE: A short path from creating your account to reading your first AI recommendation.
GUIDE.STEP1_DESC: Sign up with your email to get your own Smart Irrigation account.
GUIDE.STEP2_DESC: Each device is registered with a unique API key, configured on the ESP32 firmware so it can report readings securely.
GUIDE.STEP3_DESC: On the Pump Control page, set your soil moisture threshold and irrigation duration.
GUIDE.STEP4_DESC: Check your Dashboard for real-time soil moisture, temperature and humidity readings.
GUIDE.STEP5_DESC: Decide how this zone should be irrigated, and switch modes at any time from Pump Control.
GUIDE.STEP6_DESC: Review the Alerts and History pages to see what happened and when.
GUIDE.STEP7_DESC: Check the recommendation on your Dashboard for a quick read on current conditions.
GUIDE.NOTE: Covers account setup, the dashboard, automatic and manual irrigation, alerts, and troubleshooting.

FAQ.TITLE: Frequently asked questions
FAQ.A1: Check three things: the current soil moisture may already be above your configured threshold (Automatic Mode), the zone may be in Manual Mode waiting for you to start it yourself, or the device may not have sent a recent reading — the Pump Control page flags this as an offline/stale warning.
FAQ.A2: The system compares each new soil moisture reading to the threshold you configured in Pump Control. If moisture is below the threshold, the pump starts; it stops automatically once your configured duration is reached.
FAQ.Q3: What happens if the sensor is offline?
FAQ.A3: The Dashboard and Pump Control pages show a clear warning when no recent reading has arrived. Automatic Mode simply won't make new decisions until fresh data comes in, and a periodic safety check still makes sure the pump can't be left running indefinitely.
FAQ.A4: Yes — switch to Manual Mode on Pump Control and use Start/Stop directly. While Automatic Mode is active, manual actions are still subject to the configured safety duration.
FAQ.A5: Alerts are created automatically by the system when irrigation starts or completes, when a safety stop occurs, or when soil moisture or connectivity needs your attention. You can review them all on the Alerts page.
FAQ.A6: The platform can register more than one device. Today's Dashboard and Pump Control screens are built around managing one zone at a time per session.
